questions.prompt: Which preposition means 'in' when talking about a city?
questions.explanation: 'U' means 'in' and is used with cities and enclosed spaces: u Zagrebu, u kući.

questions.prompt: 'Stanujem u ___.' — Which form is correct for 'Zagreb'?
questions.explanation: After 'u' (in), we need the locative case. Zagreb → Zagrebu.

questions.prompt: What does 'stan' mean?
questions.explanation: Stan = apartment/flat. Kuća = house. Soba = room. Zgrada = building.

questions.prompt: Which sentence means 'I live in a big city'?
questions.explanation: After 'u' + locative: 'veliki' becomes 'velikom', 'grad' becomes 'gradu'.

questions.prompt: 'Na' + locative is used with which of these?
questions.explanation: 'Na' is used with islands, open surfaces, and some specific places: na otoku, na plaži, na poslu.

questions.prompt: What is the locative form of 'Hrvatska' after 'u'?
questions.explanation: Hrvatska → Hrvatskoj in the locative. Feminine nouns ending in -a change to -oj.

questions.prompt: What does 'lijevo' mean?
questions.explanation: Lijevo = left. Desno = right. Ravno = straight. Natrag = back.

questions.prompt: Stanujem u ___. (Split, locative)
questions.explanation: Split → Splitu in the locative case (masculine nouns add -u).

questions.prompt: Živim u lijepoj ___. (kuća, locative)
questions.hint: Feminine nouns ending in -a change to -i in locative.
questions.explanation: Kuća → kući in the locative. The adjective 'lijepa' becomes 'lijepoj' to match.

questions.prompt: Moj stan je u ___ katu. (drugi = second, locative)
questions.explanation: Drugi → drugom in the locative (masculine adjective ending -om).

questions.prompt: Škola je ___ od bolnice. (right)
questions.explanation: Desno = right/to the right. Desno od = to the right of.

questions.prompt: Oni žive na ___. (otok = island, locative)
questions.explanation: Otok → otoku in the locative. 'Na' is used with islands.

questions.prompt: Idem ___. (straight)
questions.explanation: Ravno = straight (ahead). Used for directions: Idite ravno = Go straight.

questions.prompt: Gdje stanujete?
questions.explanation: Gdje = where. Stanujete = you live (formal, from stanovati).

questions.prompt: Živim u malom stanu u centru grada.
questions.correctAnswer: I live in a small apartment in the city center.
acceptableAnswers: I live in a small flat in the city centre.
I live in a small apartment in the center of the city.
questions.explanation: Malom stanu = small apartment (locative). Centru grada = city center (locative + genitive).

questions.prompt: Pošta je lijevo od banke.
questions.correctAnswer: The post office is to the left of the bank.
acceptableAnswers: The post office is left of the bank.
questions.explanation: Pošta = post office. Lijevo od = to the left of. Banka → banke (genitive after 'od').

questions.prompt: Moja kuća ima veliki vrt.
questions.correctAnswer: My house has a big garden.
acceptableAnswers: My house has a large garden.
My home has a big garden.
questions.explanation: Moja kuća = my house. Ima = has. Veliki vrt = big garden.

questions.prompt: Idite ravno, pa onda desno.
questions.explanation: Idite = go (formal imperative). Ravno = straight. Pa onda = then. Desno = right.

questions.prompt: I live in Croatia.
questions.explanation: Hrvatska → Hrvatskoj (locative after 'u'). Živim = I live.

questions.prompt: The school is next to the park.
questions.explanation: Pored/pokraj = next to (+ genitive). Park → parka in genitive.

questions.prompt: Where is the hospital?
questions.explanation: Gdje = where, je = is, bolnica = hospital.

questions.prompt: We live on an island.
questions.explanation: 'Na' is used with islands. Otok → otoku (locative).

questions.prompt: Turn left at the traffic light.
questions.explanation: Skrenite = turn (formal imperative). Na semaforu = at the traffic light (locative).

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'živjeti' (to live) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja živim, ti živiš, on/ona/ono živi, mi živimo, vi živite, oni/one/ona žive
questions.hint: Despite the -jeti infinitive, this conjugates like an -im verb.
questions.explanation: Živjeti → živim. Many -jeti verbs follow the -im pattern in the present tense.
questions.exampleSentence: Živimo u lijepom gradu na obali.
questions.exampleTranslation: We live in a beautiful city on the coast.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'stanovati' (to reside/live) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja stanujem, ti stanuješ, on/ona/ono stanuje, mi stanujemo, vi stanujete, oni/one/ona stanuju
questions.hint: This is a -ovati verb — the -ova- becomes -uje- in the present.
questions.explanation: Stanovati → stanujem. Verbs ending in -ovati change to -ujem in present tense.
questions.exampleSentence: Stanujemo u novom stanu blizu centra.
questions.exampleTranslation: We reside in a new apartment near the center.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'ići' (to go) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja idem, ti ideš, on/ona/ono ide, mi idemo, vi idete, oni/one/ona idu
questions.hint: This is an irregular verb — the stem changes completely.
questions.explanation: Ići → idem. The infinitive stem (ić-) is replaced by id- in the present tense.
questions.exampleTranslation: I go to school every day.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'putovati' (to travel) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja putujem, ti putuješ, on/ona/ono putuje, mi putujemo, vi putujete, oni/one/ona putuju
questions.hint: Another -ovati verb — same pattern as stanovati.
questions.explanation: Putovati → putujem. The -ova- becomes -uje- in all present tense forms.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'tražiti' (to look for/search) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja tražim, ti tražiš, on/ona/ono traži, mi tražimo, vi tražite, oni/one/ona traže
questions.hint: This is a standard -im conjugation verb.
questions.explanation: Tražiti follows the regular -im pattern like raditi.
questions.exampleSentence: Tražim ulicu Ilica. Gdje je to?
questions.exampleTranslation: I'm looking for Ilica street. Where is that?
